title: barleria coriacea subsp. coriacea
intro: Barleria coriacea subsp. coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the rough barleria or hairy barleria. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Barleria coriacea subsp. coriacea include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tonics, and ointments.
